findings.summary: I2C pull-up detection misses resistors connected through a closed solder jumper; Component count, types, and BOM extraction are accurate for the Micro design; U1 LIS3DH pin mapping and decoupling c...

correct.description: The analyzer correctly identifies 25 total components with the right breakdown: 4 resistors (R1, R2, R3, R6), 2 capacitors (C3, C4), 1 LED (D1), 1 IC (U1 LIS3DH), 3 jumpers (JP1, JP2, JP3), 3 connectors (J1, J2, J5), 4 test points (TP5, TP6, TP7, TP8), 2 fiducials (FID3, FID4), 3 aesthetic symbols (G1, G2, G3), 1 mounting hole (ST1), and 1 OSHW logo (G4 not-in-BOM). Power rails 3.3V and GND are correctly identified. 17 nets and 5 no-connects are correct.

The analyzer correctly identifies U1's VDD (pin 14) and VDDIO (pin 1) on 3.3V with decoupling caps C3 (0.1uF) and C4 (10uF). GND pins 5 and 12 are on GND. RES (pin 10) tied to GND is correctly captured. ADC1/ADC2/ADC3 pins left unconnected are correctly noted. Total decoupling of 10.1uF on 3.3V rail is correctly computed.

SCL is correctly classified as 'clock', SDA as 'data', ADDR/SDO as 'data', INT1 as 'interrupt', 3.3V as 'power', GND as 'ground'. The 10 unnamed single-ended nets are classified as 'signal', which is reasonable for the no-connect and internal jumper nets.

The analyzer correctly identifies U1 (LIS3DH) as an IMU/motion sensor. The subcircuit analysis correctly groups all neighboring components (C3, C4, J1, J2, JP1, JP2, JP3, R1, R2, R6, TP5, TP6, TP7) around U1. The LIS3DH is a triple-axis accelerometer which is a type of IMU/motion sensor.

The analyzer correctly shows R2 (10k) connected between ADDR/SDO net (U1 pin 7) and 3.3V, acting as an address pull-up. JP3 (open jumper) allows optionally pulling ADDR/SDO to GND to change the I2C address. The signal pin analysis for pin 7 correctly notes R2 as a series resistor to 3.3V net.

The design_observations entry for decoupling_coverage on 3.3V correctly identifies has_bulk=true (C4 at 10uF) and has_bypass=true (C3 at 0.1uF). has_high_freq=false is correct since there is no ceramic cap in the nF range. Total 10.1uF is correctly computed.

incorrect.description: The Micro design has R1 (2.2k) on SDA and R6 (2.2k) on SCL as I2C pull-up resistors. Both connect from the I2C signal net through JP2 (a 3-pole closed/bridged solder jumper, value 'I2C') to 3.3V. The analyzer reports has_pullup=false and pull_ups=[] for both SCL and SDA in design_observations and bus_analysis. The resistors do appear on the I2C nets in ic_pin_analysis but the pull-up topology is not recognized. The jumper intervenes between the resistor free end and the 3.3V rail, creating an indirect path the analyzer does not traverse.

missed.description: The bus_analysis.i2c entries for SCL and SDA report pull_ups=[] even though R6 (2.2k) and R1 (2.2k) are clearly acting as pull-up resistors for SCL and SDA respectively. The resistors are connected in series from the signal net through JP2 (closed) to 3.3V. A correct analysis would list R6 and R1 as pull-up resistors with value 2.2k and rail 3.3V.

findings.summary: I2C pull-up detection misses pull-up resistors on SCL and SDA in the Qwiic 1x1 design; Component count and type classification are accurate for the Qwiic 1x1 design; Qwiic 1x1 design correctly iden...

correct.description: The analyzer correctly identifies 29 total components: 5 resistors (R1-R5), 2 capacitors (C1, C2), 1 LED (D1), 1 IC (U1 LIS3DH), 3 jumpers (JP1-JP3), 5 connectors (J1-J5), 4 fiducials (FID1-FID4), 4 mounting holes (ST1-ST4), and 4 aesthetic/logo symbols. 19 nets, 0 no-connects, and power rails 3.3V/GND are all correctly reported.

The Qwiic 1x1 design has two Qwiic connectors (J3 and J5) plus a standard 5-pin PTH header (J2) and a 4-pin I2C header (J4). The analyzer correctly identifies J3 and J5 as Qwiic connectors (JST 1mm 4-pin) and J4 as an I2C connector. Both Qwiic connectors are on the same I2C bus, enabling daisy-chaining.

incorrect.description: The Qwiic 1x1 design has R3 (2.2k) on SCL and R2 (2.2k) on SDA as I2C pull-ups, connected through JP2 (closed jumper) to 3.3V. The analyzer reports has_pullup=false and pull_ups=[] for both I2C lines. The resistors are visible in the net topology (R3 pin 1 on SCL net, R2 pin 1 on SDA net) and their other pins connect to unnamed nets that feed through JP2 to 3.3V. The indirect path through the jumper prevents the pull-up from being detected.

All 24 unique component references appear in missing_mpn, which is accurate: the SparkFun custom library symbols do not embed MPN values. The analyzer correctly reports this. This is a design data quality observation, not an analyzer error.

findings.summary: Board dimensions, layer stack, and routing statistics are accurate for the Micro PCB; Net list in PCB matches schematic nets accurately; SMD count (17) understates actual SMD components; THT count ...

correct.description: The analyzer correctly reports: board 19.05mm x 7.62mm (standard SparkFun Micro form factor), 2 copper layers (F.Cu, B.Cu), 35 footprints, 92 track segments, 15 vias, 1 zone, 17 nets, routing_complete=true, 0 unrouted nets. SMD count 17 and THT count 2 (J1 and J5 are PTH connectors, standoff ST1 is another) — actually smd_count=17 and tht_count=2 seems low given ST1 is a standoff hole but board_only items may be excluded.

The PCB net list correctly contains 17 nets matching the schematic: 3.3V, GND, SDA, SCL, ADDR/SDO, INT1, Net-(D1-A), Net-(JP1-A), Net-(JP2-A), Net-(JP2-C), and 5 unconnected-* nets for U1 unused pins plus 2 unconnected Qwiic NC pins. The unconnected nets for ADC1/ADC2/ADC3/CS/INT2 on U1 and the NC pins on J2 correctly match the schematic no-connects.

incorrect.description: The schematic has 11 real SMD components (U1, C3, C4, D1, R1, R2, R3, R6, JP1, JP2, JP3, J2, FID3, FID4) plus logo footprints. The PCB reports smd_count=17 and tht_count=2. The tht_count=2 appears to count only J1 and J5 (the two PTH pin connectors), while ST1 (standoff) may be excluded or treated differently. This count is likely correct for the actual through-hole pads present, but is worth noting as the standoff footprint type may vary.

findings.summary: Qwiic 1x1 PCB statistics are accurately extracted

correct.description: The analyzer correctly reports: board 25.4mm x 25.4mm (SparkFun 1x1 inch Qwiic form factor), 67 footprints (many board-only logos/graphics), 139 track segments, 41 vias, 1 zone, 19 nets, routing_complete=true, 0 unrouted nets, 2 copper layers. The higher via count (41 vs 15 in Micro) makes sense for the larger, more complex board.
